summary: The validation story is not a random badge wall. It shows Bloombase repeatedly entering the platforms where enterprise buyers actually evaluate deployment and interoperability.

items.label: Dell certification
items.note: Early dated proof that interoperability was being framed as a certification milestone, not just a product claim.

items.label: HPE Helion / ArcSight
items.note: Shows cloud and security-stack compatibility arriving together in the same chapter.

items.label: IBM security validation
items.note: A stronger maturity signal that the story remained legible beyond early alliance announcements.

items.label: VMware Cloud on AWS
items.note: Evidence that Bloombase kept translating into newer cloud deployment surfaces.

items.label: Azure Marketplace
items.note: Public-cloud distribution proof, not just classic enterprise partnership language.

summary: The strongest middle-years pattern is trust infrastructure: key management, HSM adjacency, and cryptographic ecosystems where novelty matters less than assurance.

items.label: HP Enterprise Secure Key Manager
items.note: A practical interoperability signal in the key-management layer.

items.label: Thales ASAP alliance
items.note: Places Bloombase inside a recognizable encryption and cloud-security ecosystem.

items.label: nCipher collaboration
items.note: Extends the trust story into another security-hardware-aligned relationship.

items.label: Futurex partnership
items.note: Shows cryptographic trust expansion continuing into the next infrastructure cycle.

items.label: Utimaco alliance
items.note: Reopens the same trust theme in explicitly AI-era language.

summary: What matters editorially is the accumulation pattern: storage, virtualization, SIEM, cloud marketplaces, and accelerator-era framing all connect back to the same durability thesis.

items.label: OpenStack community
items.note: Earliest strong public proof that the company wanted to matter inside cloud infrastructure.

items.label: VMware alliance
items.note: A founding-year signal that virtualization-era trust was part of the thesis from the start.

items.label: Hitachi Data Systems
items.note: Adds breadth across enterprise storage environments.

items.label: NVIDIA DPU / GPU security framing
items.note: Shows the same core problem being reframed for accelerated computing rather than abandoned.
